title: arenaria montana subsp. linearifolia
intro: Arenaria montana subsp. linearifolia, also known as mountain sandwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to Europe and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of glycosides and terpenoids. The health benefits of mountain sandwort include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and preventing infections, making it a popular remedy for skin issues and injuries. Herbal preparations of mountain sandwort include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
